title: Qwen3-14B INT4 (OpenVINO GenAI) in-process release/reload memory behavior on Intel Arc 140V (Lunar Lake)
question: Does an in-process OpenVINO GenAI LLMPipeline release (drop all refs + one gc.collect) return the integrated-GPU shared-memory carve-out WITHOUT a process restart, on Lunar Lake? (ref: openvinotoolkit/openvino #33896 — iGPU memory not auto-released on idle)

method.build: build one LLMPipeline (target + draft) on GPU, warm with one generation
method.cycle: measure -> unload (del refs + 1x gc.collect) -> 60s stability-gated settle (stop when 3 consecutive per-PID GPU reads within 100 MB) -> measure -> reload (fresh LLMPipeline build) -> coherence generation
configs: B2 same unload inside a long-lived process with full warmed allocator state (authoritative for #33896)

counters.gpu_per_process: \GPU Process Memory(*)\{Local Usage, Shared Usage, Total Committed} filtered by pid_<PID>_
counters.ram_genuineness_crosscheck: \Memory\Committed Bytes and \Memory\Free & Zero Page List Bytes (rules out standby-cache inflation)

result.iGPU_local_equals_shared: On this integrated GPU the per-process Local Usage and Shared Usage counters report the same value (unified memory); the model weights also appear in the process Working Set.

load_peak_measured_blarai_down.av_heavy_features_off_retest: Re-ran with Bitdefender heavy real-time features disabled: min Available 19 MB, Committed peak 29.1 GB (UNCHANGED), max pages-in 179k — essentially identical. The thrash is inherent to the dual CPU+GPU weight allocation, NOT antivirus file-scanning.
load_peak_measured_blarai_down.note: Available dips transiently to ~67 MB (19 MB AV-off), Committed peaks 29.1 GB, pages-in ~180-206k/s for ~6s; the load COMPLETES (~32-36s to READY) and runs at 37.4 tok/s but is MARGINAL. It exhausts PHYSICAL RAM headroom (Available -> ~0): the 31.3 GB figure is PHYSICAL RAM, the commit LIMIT is higher (pagefile). No OOM (commit limit not hit), but physical exhaustion IS the page-storm. Correction of an earlier note: 'Committed 29.1 < 31.3 so RAM never exhausted' conflated commit-limit with physical headroom — physical WAS exhausted.

verdict_three_cases.1_14b_release: SOUND — #33896 doesn't bite; ~10 GB iGPU back in ~1s, genuine (committed-corroborated), reversible, coherent reload. Directly measured (B2 live process).
verdict_three_cases.2_30b_standalone_load_leaned_box: WORKS BUT MARGINAL — the operator's daily case; loads + runs 37.4 t/s from a leaned ~25 GB box, but barely (Available -> 67/19 MB, 29.1 GB committed, ~6s page-storm = physical exhaustion).
verdict_three_cases.3_the_actual_swap: MEASURED end-to-end (live BlarAI -> release the 14B in-process -> measure the resulting headroom, from a real un-leaned ~19.9 GB cold ambient): the BARE swap (BlarAI stays alive) lands at 20.1 GB Available (releasing the 14B freed +11.3 GB; backend GPU 8.7 -> 0.65 GB; unload 1.4s). 20.1 < 23.1 GB (lowest measured successful 30B load) -> SUB-THRESHOLD: the 30B load was correctly NOT attempted (it would death-spiral) and the gate aborts. Swap-BACK proven: the 14B reloads fresh in the live process in ~21s, coherent (GPU back to 11 GB). So the bare swap (release only the 14B) is NOT viable — BlarAI staying resident keeps it ~2 GB below even the cold baseline. From this ambient even a FULL BlarAI teardown (~19.9 GB cold) is still sub-threshold unless non-BlarAI apps are also trimmed.
verdict_three_cases.conditional_verdict: Release proven; standalone load proven-but-marginal; the BARE swap is NOT viable (measured 20.1 GB, sub-threshold). The swap is viable ONLY IF it ACTIVELY reaches >=~23-25 GB before the 30B load (full BlarAI step-aside AND ambient trim) and VERIFIES it. The pre-load headroom check is the LOAD-BEARING gate: from a real ambient it ABORTS (as it correctly did here), which is the whole point.
surfaced_facts: start-llm's 21 GB gate is TOO LOW (measured load peak 29.1 GB committed) AND lets the bare swap through (20.1 >= 21? no — 20.1 < 21, but a leaner-ambient bare swap ~22 passes the 21 gate yet is still < the 23.1 it needs)
the 14B is HOST-side on the Arc 140V iGPU, not in the VM
verdict_three_cases.milestone_2_gate: Dispatch wiring (milestone 2) gating is now satisfiable-but-conditional: the bare swap is measured-not-viable; a viable swap requires the active step-aside + ambient-trim path, whose 30B LOAD has not been run from a >=23 GB swap-achieved ambient (deferred — would just reproduce the marginal standalone case).

not_measured: The full live-release-then-30B co-state in one process tree (the swap-state load was inferred from the BlarAI-down peak vs B2's measured ~22 GB post-release Available, not run directly).
A genuinely lean production ambient (~27-28 GB free) where the load might avoid thrashing.
takeaway: On this Arc 140V / driver 32.0.101.8826 / OpenVINO 2026.1.0 stack, dropping all references to an OpenVINO GenAI LLMPipeline plus a single gc.collect() returns the full iGPU shared-memory carve-out (~10 GB) within ~1 second, stably and reversibly, with no process restart and no driver-held residue — i.e. #33896 does not manifest here. The freed memory is genuine (Committed Bytes drops in lockstep). A second gc.collect() is unnecessary.
